title: Neck pain

red_flags.text: Requires red-flag identification and lists myelopathic symptoms, fever, immunosuppression, weight loss, cancer, systemic symptoms, chest symptoms, and significant trauma.
red_flags.rationale: Directly supports mapped neck-pain concern and specific-negative documentation.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Requires red-flag identification and lists myelopathic symptoms, fever, immunosuppression, weight loss, cancer, systemic symptoms, chest symptoms, and significant trauma.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Neck Pain – 22

red_flags.text: States that atraumatic neck pain without red flags generally needs no imaging and reserves radiography, CT, or MRI for red flags, progressive neurology, or persistent moderate-to-severe pain.
red_flags.rationale: Directly supports mapped radiography and MRI review only in selected concern or persistent-symptom contexts.
red_flags.evidence_extract: States that atraumatic neck pain without red flags generally needs no imaging and reserves radiography, CT, or MRI for red flags, progressive neurology, or persistent moderate-to-severe pain.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Neck Pain – 22

examination.text: Supports onset, duration, pain character, arm radiation, paraesthesia, activity impact, posture, movement, range of motion, palpation, neurological assessment, gait, and provocative-manoeuvre documentation.
examination.rationale: Directly supports mapped onset, duration, radiation, paraesthesia, function, posture, range, palpation, gait, and neurological examination fields.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports onset, duration, pain character, arm radiation, paraesthesia, activity impact, posture, movement, range of motion, palpation, neurological assessment, gait, and provocative-manoeuvre documentation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Neck Pain – 22

exact_section.heading: 7. Clinical History and face-to-face examination elements

examination.text: Lists trauma, persistent symptoms, headache, numbness or tingling, lower-limb weakness, gait or coordination issues, bowel or bladder dysfunction, immunosuppression, weight loss, fever, and chest symptoms.
examination.rationale: Directly supports mapped trauma, neurological, fever, weight-loss, worsening, and clinician-arranged review items.
examination.evidence_extract: Lists trauma, persistent symptoms, headache, numbness or tingling, lower-limb weakness, gait or coordination issues, bowel or bladder dysfunction, immunosuppression, weight loss, fever, and chest symptoms.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Neck Pain – 22

source_limitations: The legacy cervical-strain label, torticollis aliases, shoulder-abduction test, analgesia, ergonomic advice, and fixed follow-up periods remain unsupported by this mapping.
No diagnosis or normal neurological finding may be inferred.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
